user_message: Working on Woodgrove Bank's competitive positioning. Got the email thread -- help me draft the follow-up.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Rachel Berg <rachel.berg@woodgroveban.com>
To: Jennifer Walsh <jennifer.walsh@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-02 12:03 EST
Subject: Re: Re: competitive positioning Timeline

There's been a change in our timeline for competitive positioning. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Jennifer Walsh <jennifer.walsh@microsoft.com>
To: Rachel Berg <rachel.berg@woodgroveban.com>
Date: 2024-02-21 17:37 EST
Subject: URGENT: competitive positioning Issue at Woodgrove Bank

Our team encountered some challenges with competitive positioning. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Rachel Berg <rachel.berg@woodgroveban.com>
To: Jennifer Walsh <jennifer.walsh@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-04 17:26 EST
Subject: Follow-up: competitive positioning Review

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with competitive positioning. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Woodgrove Bank: there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us

handoffs.condition: MSX records need dark-period hygiene catchup

usability_anchors: Score 5: Perfect execution of the golden-path workflow with all artifacts complete and actionable
Score 3: Core output correct but missing polish or one secondary artifact
Score 1: Fails to produce expected output for a clean, unambiguous request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: All evidence correctly sourced and attributed; no hallucination
Score 3: Minor attribution gap but no fabrication
Score 1: Evidence fabricated or significantly misattributed
decision_anchors: Score 5: All decisions correct for this straightforward scenario
Score 3: Primary decision correct; minor secondary issue
Score 1: Primary decision wrong despite clear inputs

guardrail_checks: Clean golden-path scenario -- all guardrails should pass trivially
Must not over-complicate a straightforward request
